text: Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
Meeting called to order by Chair Donald Bisson at 7:02 p.m.
Present: Donald Bisson, Brenda Morissette, Roger Oblin, Jeff Laferriere, Anna Turner
Be it resolved that the Temiskaming Shores Public Library Board accepts the agenda as
OLA/FOPL Budget Ask. Motion Correspondence b.
Social Enterprise Round Table. Motion New Business b.

Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
Be it resolved that the Temiskaming Shores Public Library Board approves the minutes of
the meeting held on Wednesday, January 17, 2018, as presented.
Roger Oblin inquired if the circulation desk in Haileybury had been repaired. The CEO a.
confirmed that it had been repaired.
Barbara Franchetto, CEO—Southern Ontario Library Service. Report LIB- a. From:
Re: Invitation to Library Leaders Focus Group on March 16, 2018, all expenses paid.
Be it resolved that the Temiskaming Shores Public Library Board accepts report LIB-
004-2018 Library Leaders Focus Group and recommends sending Library CEO
Rebecca Hunt to the Library Leaders Focus Group meeting in Toronto in March,
Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
Stephen Abram, Executive Director—Federation of Ontario Public Libraries. b. From:
Re: Local city and town council motions supporting the OLA/FOPL Budget Ask. OLA
and FOPL are asking the province to increase the operating grant to public libraries.
They request that the board endorse the proposal and encourage the municipal
council to pass a resolution supporting the “Ask.” Discussion.
Be it resolved that the Temiskaming Shores Public Library Board endorses the OLA
and FOPL’s recommendations in their pre-budget submission and refers the
correspondence to the City of Temiskaming Shores for resolution of Council to
Report and monthly financial statement included in the trustees’ information packet
were conducted in the library buildings. Fire safety checks
Digital Digital Creator North (Near North Mobile Media Lab Trillium project):
Creator has been keeping busy with catering to the teen participants wants and needs.
Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
Giving workshops that they suggest such as "Sports Coverage at TDSS" and "Digital
Sketch Club" Digital Creator has been bridging the relationship gap and giving the
teens more control of how the space could be run. Digital Creator also hosted guest
artist "Alexander Rondeau" to give a workshop on "Queering the Normative and Art of
the Readymade" which gained attention from TDSS's "LGBTQ Alliance" the workshop
was a very positive experience for Digital Creator and is looking forward to doing more
I attended the annual general meeting Federation of Ontario Public Libraries board:
and regular board meeting on Thursday, February 1 in person at the Ontario Library
Association conference in Toronto. We now have three representative librarians from
Northeastern Ontario, as Samara Martel from Earlton library was appointed to the Board
in addition to myself and Christina Blazecka from Cochrane library.
The first few Healthy Kids Community Challenge #PowerOffAndPlay Program:
Games, Storytime and Active play time sessions at the New Liskeard Branch were
successful, with 17 attending the first session and 19 attending the second. The Healthy
Kids Coordinator and staff from the Haileybury branch have chosen about $1000 worth
of equipment and games for the Haileybury Branch so we can start a program there as
well. The Haileybury Power off and Play program will take place on Wednesday
afternoons after school and will start up after Family Day.
the January meeting Joint Automation Server Initiative (JASI) Steering Committee:
was cancelled, but I will attend the next teleconference meeting set for Wednesday,
March 28. The committee is still discussing cataloguing standards and item types.
I attended the conference from January 30- Ontario Library Association Conference:
February 3, and provided transportation for the librarians from Englehart and Elk Lake. I
found all of the sessions I attended to be useful, especially the sessions on library space
Ontario Library Service North Ontario Library Service North Mentorship Program:
has asked me to Mentor an additional Librarian from the North, Dayna Debenedet from
Dryden. I met with Dayna at OLA and will be touching base with her later in February.
The Games and The Temiskaming Foundation Games and Backpacks program:
Backpacks are in circulation. In January there were 11 circulations of the Games and
three circulations of the backpacks. We have also had 15 circulations of the snowshoes
at the Haileybury branch so far this year. In addition to the article in the Temiskaming
Speaker, I also did a short interview for the CJTT news on Monday February 5, which
aired during the day on February 6.
Our Adult volunteer and Co-op Student continue to log hours at Volunteer Program:
the Haileybury Branch. As well, our Adult volunteers continue to shelf-read and take
Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
books to the Manor at the New Liskeard Branch. We have an additional three adult
volunteers in New Liskeard, two of them logging hours for educational programs.
Visits to the Extendicare, Lodge and Manor nursing homes to exchange books
Ongoing on Fridays and Saturdays by staff members and volunteers.
Life Skills visits at the New Liskeard Branch
The Life Skills group continues to visit every second Wednesday for a storytime and craft
Gadget Helper at both branches of the library
The Gadget Helper program has been fully booked for the January and February dates
Blind Date with a Book at Both Branches of the Library
Have your Date February 1-14 and email your review by February 16!
Three age categories: Youth (ages 8-12), Teen (ages 13-18) and Adult. Check out one
of the wrapped books for a Blind Date between February 1 and 14, then email or drop
off your book review to us at NewLiskeard@temisklibrary.com by February 16 for a
chance to win a prize! For more information please call us 705-647-4215 or 705-672-
We hope you all enjoy your blind dates with your books and we are very much looking
forward to reading your blind date book reviews. Winners will be announced on the 20
Digital Creator Workshop in the Digital Creator Space, New Liskeard Branch
Youth of all ages are welcome to join in for this workshop in which we will be learning
about how to/make our own stop-motion animations.
Please email Steve at temiskamingshores@digitalcreator.ca for more information and
Power Off and Play at the Haileybury Branch
Join us at the Haileybury Branch Library on Wednesday, February 21 and every
second Wednesday until May 2 for story time, crafts and games!
Call 705-672-3707 or stop by either branch to sign up!
Meeting Minutes of the Temiskaming Shores February 21, 2018
Family Story, Game and Play Time at the New Liskeard Branch
The Library is partnering with the Healthy Kids Community Challenge
#PowerOffAndPlay to provide a bi-weekly family story and play time to encourage
screen free time for parents and kids! The next session is on
2:00—2:45 p.m.--board games to be played/pages to colour
2:45—3:00 p.m.—active play time with different apparatus
This will be a fun time for the whole family! Call 705-647-4215 for more information
Digital Creator Workshop in the Digital Creator Space, New Liskeard Branch
Youth of all ages are welcome to join in for this workshop in which we will be learning
about how to light, shoot and edit Green Screen footage.
Please email Steve at temiskamingshores@digitalcreator.ca for more information and

Temiskaming Shores Public Library Bookclub at Both Branches of the Library
The Book Pick for March is Madeleine L’Engle’s “A Wrinkle in Time”—just in time for
the movie to come out! The library has two copies and we can order more through
Inter-Library Loan! We will meet to discuss the book in early March.
